goToPath.dialog.title: Go to path

@goToPath.dialog.title.description: Title of the "Go to path" dialog (opened with Command-G), which jumps a pane to a typed filesystem location. "Path" means a filesystem location like ~/Documents.

@goToPath.dialog.inputAriaLabel.description: Accessibility label (read by screen readers) for the path input in the "Go to path" dialog.

goToPath.dialog.inputPlaceholder: Type or paste a path, e.g. ~/Documents

@goToPath.dialog.inputPlaceholder.description: Placeholder in the "Go to path" dialog's input. "~/Documents" is an example filesystem path; keep it literally as a sample.

@goToPath.dialog.recentsAriaLabel.description: Accessibility label (read by screen readers) for the list of recently visited paths in the "Go to path" dialog.

@goToPath.dialog.removeFromList.description: Tooltip and accessibility label for the button that removes one entry from the recent-paths list. A short label.

@goToPath.dialog.cancel.description: Button that closes the "Go to path" dialog without navigating. A short button label.

@goToPath.dialog.confirm.description: Primary button in the "Go to path" dialog that navigates to the typed path. A short button label.

goToPath.dialog.ancestorHint: This path doesn''t exist. The closest place to go is {dir}.

@goToPath.dialog.ancestorHint.description: Inline warning under the input when the typed path doesn't exist; tells the user the nearest existing folder it would go to instead.

placeholders.dir: the nearest existing parent folder's path (e.g. /Users/me)

goToPath.toast.landedOnAncestor: <req>{requested}</req> doesn''t exist, so we took you to <land>{landed}</land>.

@goToPath.toast.landedOnAncestor.description: Toast shown after "Go to path" navigated to the nearest existing folder because the typed path was missing. Both paths are wrapped in tags (<req></req> and <land></land>) that render them in a monospace font; keep the tags around each path placeholder.

placeholders.requested: the path the user typed, which doesn't exist (e.g. /tmp/nope)
placeholders.landed: the nearest existing folder we navigated to instead (e.g. /tmp)

@goToPath.toast.pressToGoBack.description: Second line of the nearest-ancestor toast, telling the user which shortcut returns to the previous location. The <chip></chip> tag is replaced by a key-combination chip (e.g. ⌘[); keep the empty tag where the chip should appear.
